summary: Obfuscated dropper exfiltrates host identifiers and fetches remote payload on require()
details: The package presents itself as an HTTP URL-encoding utility but its sole file (index.js) is a heavily obfuscated dropper. On module load, a top-level call (Zt()) decodes XOR-encoded strings (key [0x70,0xa0,0x89,0x48]) and obfuscator.io string-array entries to recover shell commands and paths ('.vscode', 'f.js', 'cd', '&& npm i --silent', 'nohup', 'node_modules', 'package.json'), GETs a remote payload from a hardcoded C2 whose IP is reassembled from base64 fragments (e.g., 'MTY1LjE0'+'OTA='), writes the payload to ~/.vscode/f.js, runs `npm i --silent` inside that directory, and spawns the script detached via `nohup node...` using child_process. Before/with execution, host fingerprint data — os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, os.platform() — is POSTed to the same C2 as {ts, type, hid, ss, cc}. A setInterval (~615s) re-runs the routine. Package metadata reinforces the malware shape: empty description, empty author, no README, no tests, single 9.7KB obfuscated file. Any consumer that installs and requires this package is compromised at require()-time with attacker-controlled code persisted under ~/.vscode/.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
